channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: No carbon monoxide in Gene Hackman's home: Santa Fe Sheriff's Office
item.description: The Santa Fe County Sheriff's Office has announced that carbon monoxide or gas leaks were not found in the residence of late actor Gene Hackman and his wife, Betsy Arakawa, who were found dead inside their home last month.
item.dc:creator: Jacqueline Hughes, NewsNation

item.title: Scientists genetically engineer mice with thick hair like the extinct woolly mammoth
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Extinction is still forever, but scientists at the biotech company Colossal Biosciences are trying what they say is the next best thing to restoring ancient beasts — genetically engineering living animals with qualities to resemble extinct species like the woolly mammoth.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Arab leaders endorse a counterproposal to Trump's Gaza plan, with ceasefire uncertain
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Arab leaders on Tuesday endorsed Egypt's postwar plan for the Gaza Strip that would allow its roughly 2 million Palestinians to remain, in a counterproposal to U.S. President Donald Trump’s plan to depopulate the territory and redevelop it as a beach destination.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Jimmy Johnson retires from Fox Sports after being face of NFL coverage for most of 3 decades
item.description: Jimmy Johnson, the Pro Football Hall of Fame coach who won two Super Bowls and a college national championship, announced his retirement from Fox Sports on Monday after being part of its NFL coverage for most of the network's 31 years.
item.dc:creator: Eric Olson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Retired US military leaders support letting Israel 'finish the job' against Iran, proxies
item.description: Dozens of retired U.S. military leaders urge President Donald Trump to throw America's support behind Israel in its fight against a nuclear Iran.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: IHOP offers free short stack for National Pancake Day
item.description: Calling all pancake lovers! If you are ready to satisfy your craving, IHOP is celebrating National Pancake Day on March 4 with a free treat.
item.dc:creator: Odyssey Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Virginia hospital to continue gender-affirming care in light of Trump's order
item.description: The executive order was aimed at withholding federal funding for institutions who provide gender-affirming care for those under 19.
item.dc:creator: Kenzie Finch, NewsNation

item.title: Virginia daycare owner speaks after toddler wanders from facility
item.description: Monique Ede, owner of Fortress of God Childcare in Chester has spoken exclusively with 8News after a child wandered off from her facility.
item.dc:creator: Rolynn Wilson, NewsNation

item.title: More endangered Mexican gray wolves are roaming the southwestern US, annual survey shows
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — It is a ritual that takes place every winter — federal and state wildlife managers use remote cameras, scat collection, radio telemetry devices and helicopters to count Mexican gray wolves that are roaming mountain ranges in parts of New Mexico and Arizona.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fargo has an election method that helps mainstream candidates. North Dakota lawmakers may ban it
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — When Fargo voters approved a unique local election system in 2018, their goal was to choose candidates with broader support to lead North Dakota’s largest city. It worked — maybe too well.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: House Republicans advised to avoid in-person town halls amid protests
item.description: House Republicans are being encouraged to avoid in-person town halls in favor of phone and livestreamed versions, advice that comes after constituents and activists rocked recent GOP events by expressing outrage about the Trump administration’s dramatic moves to reshape the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Red Robin considering closing dozens of 'underperforming' restaurants
item.description: Red Robin, which has locations in all but five states, says it's reviewing dozens of "underperforming" stores for potential closure.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: SDSU researchers ramp up border sewage, pollution survey
item.description: "About 70 percent of households reported some form of respiratory illness and the ones that reported smelling daily odors, or at least four to five times a week, had a higher response rate of having respiratory illnesses and gastrointestinal illnesses.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: 5 things to watch as Trump delivers joint address to Congress
item.description: President Trump will head to the Capitol on Tuesday for the first joint address of his second term emboldened by a month of mass federal agency slashing, shocking foreign policy moves and a dizzying array of executive actions that have disrupted Washington and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Sanctuary city mayors, including Chicago's Brandon Johnson, to face Congress amid immigration tensions
item.description: Mayors of Chicago, NYC, Boston and Denver are preparing to defend their cities' sanctuary status, a policy that has faced increased security under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, WGN 9

item.title: 46 percent say Trump changing economy for the worse: Survey
item.description: Recent polling has indicated that U.S. adults’ confidence in Trump’s ability to manage the country’s economy — the top issue in the 2024 presidential election — has been slipping.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sheriff warns travelers of possible violence south of border after cartel extraditions
item.description: A South Texas sheriff is warning visitors to the northern Mexican state of Tamaulipas after two high ranking cartel members from Los Zetas were recently extradited from Mexico to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Wall Street's losing streak deepens as Trump's tariffs kick in
item.description: Stocks tumbled on Wall Street Tuesday as a trade war between the U.S. and its key trading partners escalated and threatened to wipe out all the gains for the S&P 500 since Election Day.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Prices rose along border ahead of Trump's tariffs
item.description: Companies that depend on cross-border trade were already facing higher prices in anticipation of the 25% tariffs on imports from Mexico and Canada that took effect Tuesday. Now disruption looms, and if consumers won’t accept steep price hikes, they’ll have to downsize operations and lay off workers.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Slain California fire captain's fugitive wife 'scared' ex mother-in-law
item.description: The former mother-in-law of a woman accused of murdering her fire captain wife in Southern California said that she was "very scary.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US stocks tumble after Trump announces Canadian and Mexican tariffs
item.description: U.S. stocks are falling sharply Monday after President Donald Trump said tariffs he announced on Canada and Mexico will take effect on Tuesday. The bond market also took a hit following the latest warning signal on the strength of the U.S. economy.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How did Americans view the Trump-Zelenskyy meeting? Poll shows stark divide
item.description: About half of those polled said neither Ukraine nor the U.S. walked away as a winner from last week's meeting.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, Fox 5

item.title: Human cannonball injured at Southern California county fair
item.description: Chachi "The Rocketman" Valencia was transported to a local hospital after he had fallen out of a safety net during the performance.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, NewsNation

item.title: Trump: 25 percent tariffs on Canada and Mexico will go into effect Tuesday
item.description: President Trump said Monday that the 25 percent tariffs on Canada and Mexico will officially go into effect Tuesday, announcing there is no room for dealmaking to delay them again. “The tariffs, they're all set.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FAA opens probe on why flights approaching Ronald Reagan airport received false alerts of nearby aircraft
item.description: The FAA has launched a probe into why flights approaching the Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport received erroneous alerts of other aircraft in the area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cat food recalled over risk of bird flu weeks after pets euthanized
item.description: The Wild Coast LLC product 16oz and 24oz frozen Boneless Free Range Chicken Formula raw pet food for cats was distributed to pet food retailers in Washington State and Oregon. The product is frozen in a small round white plastic container with a green label.
item.dc:creator: Erin Yudt, KRON4

item.title: Bondi says she was misled on Epstein documents
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi says she was misled into believing she had all the files related to the Epstein investigations, and is now seeking additional information from the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, WFLA, NBC

item.title: In Memoriam snubs: Late actors who weren't honored at the Oscars
item.description: Michelle Trachtenberg, Shannen Doherty, Tony Todd, and Quincy Jones were some of the notable figures not included in the In Memoriam segment at the 2024 Academy Awards, sparking outrage among their fans.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Kroger CEO resigns amid personal conduct investigation
item.description: McMullen's departure comes months after Kroger's failed two-year effort to merge with another supermarket giant, Albertsons.
item.dc:creator: Adam Conn, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Strip club Cinderella story 'Anora' wins best picture at 97th Academy Awards
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — "Anora,” a strip club Cinderella story without the fairy tale ending, was crowned best picture at the 97th Academy Awards on Sunday, handing Sean Baker’s gritty, Brooklyn-set screwball farce Hollywood’s top prize.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NOAA firings spark fears about long-term damage
item.description: The Trump administration has made drastic cuts to the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) that threaten to impact weather forecasting and other key services provided by the agency.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Crews from across South Carolina help battle Carolina Forest-area wildfires
item.description: Regional and state fire rescue crews on Monday continued to battle the Carolina Forest-area wildfires, according to Horry County Fire Rescue.
item.dc:creator: Jordan White, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Magnitude 4.5 earthquake strikes in Washington state
item.description: A “notable” earthquake registering at a preliminary magnitude of 4.5 struck Washington State on Monday morning, according to the United States Geological Survey.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: Singer Angie Stone killed in car crash after leaving Alabama performance
item.description: Stone was riding in the back of a vehicle that "flipped over and subsequently hit by a big rig," according to family and friends.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, ABC 4

item.title: Trump announces US crypto reserve
item.description: His announcement comes just after the White House announced it would be welcoming cryptocurrency industry professionals on March 7 in a first-of-its-kind summit.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Crews battle wildfires in North and South Carolina amid dry conditions and gusty winds
item.description: Crews battled wildfires in North and South Carolina on Sunday amid dry conditions and gusty winds as residents were forced to evacuate in some areas.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: To her, Hurricane Helene debris isn’t trash. It is full of memories
item.description: SWANNANOA, N.C. (AP) — The tops of dried, bent cornstalks crunch underfoot. Jill Holtz’s gaze is fixed on the ground ahead. She wanders into the nearby woods and weaves between twisted branches. Then, Holtz spots something and starts to riffle through the withered twigs.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, ABC 4
